content: Steel beam load calculations have important limitations. Lateral-torsional buckling can reduce capacity for unbraced beams - the AISC provides Cb factors and unbraced length limits. Local buckling of flanges or webs may occur in slender sections. Connection design is critical - simple shear connections vs moment connections dramatically affect capacity. Dynamic loads, impact, and fatigue require additional considerations beyond static analysis. Fire resistance is a major concern - steel loses 50% strength at 1100°F. Corrosion protection is essential for long-term capacity. Real-world conditions like residual stresses, initial imperfections, and erection tolerances introduce uncertainties not captured in ideal calculations.
source: AISC Steel Construction Manual & Structural Stability Research Council Guidelines
